result.reasoning: The user question requires determining which game piece receives a red peg when the opponent calls out the first move of Game 10 of the 1972 Fischer–Spassky World Chess Championship match, applied to a Battleship grid shown in an attached image. The first move from Step 3 is e4 (algebraic notation). From Step 4, the precise ship placements on the grid include a destroyer occupying coordinates E3 and E4. Step 5 confirms that coordinate E4 is occupied by that destroyer, so the call e4 results in a hit on that destroyer. Step 6 states that according to the 1990 Milton Bradley Battleship rules, when a hit occurs, a red peg is placed in the specific hole of the ship that was hit. Therefore, the game piece that receives the red peg is the destroyer. The evidence from tool outputs consistently capitalizes ship type names (e.g., "Destroyer"), so authoritative casing is used.
